summary: chain-key-validator exfiltrates host/system info to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package's package.json lifecycle script and the bundled scanner.js perform host reconnaissance and POST it to a hardcoded attacker-controlled endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io. The lifecycle script (package.json line 32) loads child_process, os, and https, gathers identifying fields (hostname, cwd, environment) and issues an HTTPS POST to that domain. scanner.js further reads the filesystem, runs whoami, collects host metadata (hostname, cwd, os version), and ships it to the same ddjidd564.github.io endpoint via https.request/https.get. The package's stated purpose (a 'chain key validator') has no legitimate need for whoami execution, host enumeration, or beaconing to a personal GitHub Pages account. This matches the canonical install-/load-time system-info exfiltration pattern and produces direct attacker benefit (installer host data delivered to attacker infrastructure).

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
